name: Negroni
method: Stir with ice, strain into rocks glass over fresh ice.

name: Boulevardier
method: Stir with ice, strain into rocks glass over fresh ice.
notes: Use bourbon or rye.

name: Old Pal
method: Stir with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Drier, sharper cousin of the Boulevardier.

name: Americano
method: Build in highball over ice, top with soda, stir gently.

name: Negroni Sbagliato
method: Build in rocks glass over ice, top with prosecco, stir gently.

name: Aperol Spritz
method: Build in wine glass over ice, stir gently.
notes: 3-2-1 ratio prosecco-Aperol-soda.

name: Campari Spritz
method: Build in wine glass over ice, stir gently.
notes: Bitterer than Aperol Spritz.

name: Martini
method: Stir with ice until very cold, strain into chilled coupe or martini glass.
notes: Ratio to taste; bitters optional but traditional.

name: Vodka Martini
method: Stir with ice, strain into chilled coupe.

name: Dirty Martini
method: Stir with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Requires olive brine.

name: Gibson
method: Stir with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Requires cocktail onion.

name: Manhattan
method: Stir with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Rye is traditional; bourbon works.

name: Rob Roy
method: Stir with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Manhattan with scotch.

name: Old Fashioned
method: Stir all with ice in rocks glass over a large cube.

name: Sazerac
method: Rinse chilled rocks glass with absinthe, discard. Stir remaining ingredients with ice, strain into glass.
notes: No ice in final glass.

name: Vieux Carré
method: Stir with ice, strain into rocks glass over fresh ice.
notes: Missing Bénédictine.

name: Whisky Sour
method: Dry shake (no ice) if using egg, then shake with ice. Strain into rocks glass over ice or into coupe.
garnish: Angostura dashes on foam, lemon peel

name: Daiquiri
method: Shake hard with ice, double strain into chilled coupe.

name: Gimlet
method: Shake with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Classic 1950s version uses cordial, not fresh juice — works directly with your Bickford's.

name: Fresh Gimlet
method: Shake with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Modern preparation.

name: Margarita
method: Shake with ice, strain into chilled rocks or coupe glass. Optional salt rim.

name: Tommy's Margarita
method: Shake with ice, strain into rocks glass over fresh ice.

name: Paloma
method: Build in highball over ice, top with grapefruit soda.
notes: Grapefruit soda not in inventory but assumed available.

name: Tequila Sour
method: Dry shake if using egg, then shake with ice. Strain into coupe.

name: Mojito
method: Gently muddle mint in shaker. Add rum, lime, syrup, ice. Shake briefly, strain into highball over ice. Top with soda.

name: Cuba Libre
method: Build in highball over ice.

name: Dark 'n' Stormy
method: Build rum and lime in highball over ice. Top with ginger beer, stir.
notes: Traditionally Gosling's; Kraken works well.

name: Mai Tai
method: Shake with crushed ice, dump into rocks glass.
notes: Missing orgeat. Trader Vic original calls for aged Jamaican + Martinique; your Santiago + Kraken/Captain Morgan OP is a fair stand-in.

name: Piña Colada
method: Blend with crushed ice, or shake hard and strain into hurricane glass.
notes: Needs coconut cream and pineapple juice.

name: Jungle Bird
method: Shake with ice, strain into rocks glass over fresh ice.
notes: Needs pineapple juice.

name: Caipirinha
method: Muddle lime and sugar in rocks glass. Add cachaça, fill with crushed ice, stir.

name: Moscow Mule
method: Build in copper mug or highball over ice.

name: Vodka Soda
method: Build in highball over ice.

name: Gin & Tonic
method: Build in highball or copa glass over ice.

name: Tom Collins
method: Shake gin, lemon, syrup with ice. Strain into collins glass over ice, top with soda.

name: French 75
method: Shake gin, lemon, syrup with ice. Strain into flute, top with sparkling wine.

name: Bee's Knees
method: Shake with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Honey syrup = honey diluted 2:1 with hot water.

name: Last Word
method: Shake with ice, double strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Needs maraschino liqueur.

name: Final Ward
method: Shake with ice, double strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Needs maraschino liqueur. Last Word variant with rye and lemon.

name: Paper Plane
method: Shake with ice, double strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Missing Amaro Nonino; Montenegro is a passable substitute.

name: Sidecar
method: Shake with ice, strain into chilled coupe.

name: Brandy Alexander
method: Shake with ice, double strain into chilled coupe.

name: Espresso Martini
method: Shake hard with ice, double strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Your First Press concentrate is purpose-made for this.

name: White Russian
method: Build vodka and Kahlúa in rocks glass over ice, float cream on top.

name: Black Russian
method: Build in rocks glass over ice, stir.

name: B-52
method: Layer carefully in shot glass: Kahlúa, then Baileys, then Grand Marnier.
notes: Substitute Cointreau or Triple Sec for Grand Marnier — close enough.

name: Godfather
method: Build in rocks glass over ice, stir.

name: Amaretto Sour
method: Dry shake if using egg, then shake with ice. Strain into rocks glass over ice.
notes: Bourbon-spiked version (Jeffrey Morgenthaler).

name: Rusty Nail
method: Build in rocks glass over ice, stir.

name: Penicillin
method: Shake scotch, lemon, honey-ginger with ice. Strain into rocks glass over ice. Float Islay on top.
notes: Honey-ginger syrup: equal parts honey, fresh ginger juice, water.

name: Rob Roy Smoky
method: Stir with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Rob Roy with peated whisky.

name: Smoky Old Fashioned
method: Stir all with ice in rocks glass over a large cube.

name: Hot Toddy
method: Combine in mug, stir until honey dissolves.

name: Irish Coffee
method: Stir whisky, coffee, sugar in heated glass. Float cream on top over a spoon.

name: Pimm's Cup
method: Build in highball over ice with cucumber, orange, mint, strawberry.

name: Cosmopolitan
method: Shake with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Needs cranberry juice. Your Smirnoff Twisted Citrus fits the spec.

name: Lemon Drop
method: Shake with ice, strain into sugar-rimmed coupe.

name: Sherry Cobbler
method: Muddle orange and syrup in shaker. Add sherry and ice, shake. Strain into wine glass packed with crushed ice.

name: Adonis
method: Stir with ice, strain into chilled coupe.

name: Bamboo
method: Stir with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Dry, low-ABV aperitif.

name: Hanky Panky
method: Stir with ice, strain into chilled coupe.

name: Toronto
method: Stir with ice, strain into rocks glass over fresh ice (or coupe).

name: Black Manhattan
method: Stir with ice, strain into chilled coupe.
notes: Missing Averna; Montenegro is a fair substitute (lighter, less bitter).

name: Montenegro Sour
method: Dry shake if using egg, then shake with ice. Strain into coupe.

name: Sloe Gin Fizz
method: Shake sloe gin, lemon, syrup with ice. Strain into highball over ice, top with soda.

name: Mint Julep
method: Gently muddle mint with syrup in julep cup. Add bourbon, fill with crushed ice, stir to chill until cup frosts.

name: Tequila Old Fashioned
method: Stir all with ice in rocks glass over a large cube.

name: Oaxaca Old Fashioned
method: Stir with ice, strain into rocks glass over a large cube.
notes: Missing mezcal — works as a tequila-only OF; add Laphroaig rinse for a similar smoky note.

name: Japanese Highball
method: Build in highball over ice (ideally large cubes), stir once briefly.

name: Bourbon Smash
method: Muddle mint and syrup in shaker. Add bourbon, lemon, ice; shake. Strain into rocks glass over crushed ice.

name: Champs-Élysées
method: Shake with ice, strain into chilled coupe.

name: Bicicletta
method: Build in wine glass over ice, top with soda.
notes: Needs white wine.

name: Garibaldi
method: Pour Campari into highball over ice. Top with vigorously aerated (blended or whipped) fresh OJ.

name: Pampelle Spritz
method: Build in wine glass over ice, stir gently.

name: Gold Rush
method: Shake with ice, strain into rocks glass over a large cube.

name: Air Mail
method: Shake rum, lime, honey with ice. Strain into flute, top with sparkling wine.

name: Tequila & Tonic
method: Build in highball over ice.

name: Toasted Almond
method: Shake with ice, strain into rocks glass over fresh ice.

name: Kir Royale
method: Pour liqueur into flute, top with sparkling wine.
notes: Cassis is traditional; Chambord works.

name: Black Manhattan
method: Stir with ice until well-chilled, strain into a chilled coupe.
notes: Todd Smith, Bourbon & Branch (2007). Averna replaces sweet vermouth.

name: Averna Sour
method: Dry shake if using egg white, then shake hard with ice. Double-strain into a coupe.
notes: Bittersweet, lower-ABV sour. Skip egg white for a punchier, sharper drink.

name: Rhubarb & Ginger G&T
method: Build over plenty of ice in a highball or copa, stir briefly.
garnish: orange wheel + sprig of rosemary or thin ginger slice
notes: Premium tonic (Fever-Tree Mediterranean or similar) recommended.

name: Rhubarb Collins
method: Shake gin, lemon and syrup with ice. Strain into a Collins glass over fresh ice, top with soda.

name: Bloody Cello Spritz
method: Build in a large wine glass over ice. Stir gently.
notes: Aperol-Spritz-style serve; the chilli warmth comes through on the finish.

name: Bloody Margarita
method: Shake hard with ice. Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice (or coupe, no ice).
notes: Bloody Cello replaces triple sec; the ancho chilli matches a Tajin-style rim.

name: Barrel-Aged Negroni (serve)
method: Pour over a large ice cube. Optional: stir for a few seconds with ice.
notes: Pre-made and rested in oak. Serve straight from the barrel/bottle.

name: Barrel-Aged Vieux Carré (serve)
method: Pour over a large ice cube. Optional: stir briefly with ice.
notes: Pre-made and rested in oak. Serve straight from the barrel/bottle.
